screenshots.heading: See it in action
screenshots.subheading: From first-launch setup to live transmission. Here's what VoxDMR looks like.
hero.subtitle: Free app for Android, Windows and Linux. Connect to DMR talkgroups on BrandMeister, TGIF and other networks straight from your phone or PC. Real AMBE+2 audio. No hotspot, no proprietary client.
features.heading: Built for DMR
features.subheading: Everything you need to get on the air, on any DMR network you can reach.
features.ptt.title: PTT Modes
features.ptt.description: Hold the spacebar or any configured key to transmit. Configurable per-server. Latch mode keeps you transmitting without holding. Useful for hands-free operation.
features.ambe.title: AMBE+2 Vocoder
features.ambe.description: Real AMBE+2 voice codec running the MD-380 firmware on x86_64. Same audio quality as a hardware DMR radio.
features.talkgroups.title: DMR Talkgroups
features.talkgroups.description: Browse and switch between thousands of talkgroups across BrandMeister and other DMR networks. Favorites, search, and live activity indicators.
features.rewind.title: Rewind Protocol
features.rewind.description: Connects via the open Rewind protocol used by hotspot firmware like Pi-Star. Just need a DMR ID and a hotspot password. No proprietary client software.
features.free.title: Free to Use
features.free.description: No account, no telemetry, no subscription. Download a release binary and start transmitting.
features.crossPlatform.title: Cross-Platform
features.crossPlatform.description: Native binaries for Linux and Windows, plus an Android app on Google Play. Same UI, same audio engine, same experience everywhere.
useCases.heading: What it's actually for
useCases.subheading: Three places VoxDMR earns its keep.
useCases.case1.title: Test the waters before buying a radio
useCases.case1.description: Try BrandMeister, listen to your local talkgroups, and check the bands are alive before dropping money on a DMR radio and hotspot.
useCases.case2.title: Travel, work, hotel rooms
useCases.case2.description: Stay on the air from anywhere with Wi-Fi or 4G. No RF, no hotspot to lug around, no antenna restrictions in the room.
useCases.case3.title: Backup when the shack's down
useCases.case3.description: Antenna repair, power outage, or just away from the rig. Keep your nets and QSOs going from your phone.
faq.heading: Questions hams ask
faq.subheading: If you're new to BrandMeister or comparing to other DMR clients, start here.
faq.q1.question: Do I need a ham license?
faq.q1.answer: Yes. VoxDMR requires a valid amateur radio license and a DMR ID issued by radioid.net. It is not for unlicensed use, and the DMR networks it connects to are licensed-amateurs-only.
faq.q2.question: How is this different from Droidstar or BlueDV?
faq.q2.answer: One app, the same UI and the same audio engine across Android, Windows and Linux. Native binaries on all three. No paid components, no separate desktop and mobile codebases.
faq.q3.question: Is the AMBE+2 vocoder legal?
faq.q3.answer: VoxDMR downloads the official MD-380 firmware from md380.org and GitHub on first launch (SHA-256 verified) and runs it in software emulation. It is the same firmware running on tens of thousands of Tytera MD-380 radios. Same legal posture as Droidstar.
faq.q4.question: Do I need a hotspot or repeater?
faq.q4.answer: No. VoxDMR connects directly to BrandMeister and other DMR servers via the open Rewind protocol. If you already have a Pi-Star hotspot you can keep using it for your handheld, but you do not need to route VoxDMR through it.
faq.q5.question: Which networks does it support?
faq.q5.answer: BrandMeister, TGIF, FreeStar, FreeDMR and any other DMR network that speaks the Rewind protocol. Server profiles let you switch between them in a tap.
cta.heading: Get on the Air
cta.description: Free to download. No account needed. Install VoxDMR on your phone or PC, enter your DMR ID, and you're on the air.
support.heading: Help keep VoxDMR free
support.description: VoxDMR runs on my time and my wallet. Real costs: the Google Play developer account, code-signing certificates for Windows releases, and the occasional VPS to test against. Tips on Ko-fi help cover those and keep new features shipping.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.overview.title: Overview
privacy.overview.text: VoxDMR is software for connecting to DMR talkgroups on BrandMeister and other DMR networks. This policy explains what data the app stores and what network connections it makes.
privacy.dataCollection.title: Data Collection
privacy.dataCollection.text: VoxDMR does not collect, store, or transmit any personal data to the developer or any third party. The app contains no analytics, advertising, or tracking of any kind.
privacy.dataStored.title: Data Stored on Your Device
privacy.dataStored.intro: The app stores the following data locally on your device only:
privacy.dataStored.serverProfilesDesc: DMR ID, hotspot password, server address, talkgroup settings, and PTT key binding. Stored in ~/.config/voxdmr/
privacy.dataStored.appPreferencesDesc: audio device selection, PTT mode, UI preferences
privacy.dataStored.outro: This data never leaves your device except when connecting directly to the DMR servers you've configured.
privacy.microphone.title: Microphone
privacy.microphone.text: VoxDMR accesses your device microphone solely for push-to-talk voice transmission to the DMR servers you connect to. Audio is encoded using the AMBE+2 codec and sent directly to the server you are connected to. Audio is not recorded, stored, or transmitted elsewhere.
privacy.network.title: Network Communication
privacy.network.intro: VoxDMR makes the following network connections:
privacy.network.tcpDesc: real-time audio and control to the DMR servers you configure
privacy.network.udpDesc: firmware download from md380.org and GitHub, SHA-256 verified. Only triggered when you click "Download" during setup.
privacy.network.outro: No other network connections are made. There is no telemetry, no update check, and no cloud service.
privacy.thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
privacy.thirdParty.text: VoxDMR does not use any third-party services, SDKs, or APIs beyond the DMR networks you connect to and firmware download sources. There are no ads, analytics, crash reporting, or cloud services.
privacy.reflectorServers.title: DMR Networks
privacy.reflectorServers.text: VoxDMR connects to DMR servers (BrandMeister and other DMR networks) operated by independent parties. The developer of VoxDMR does not operate, control, or moderate these servers. Communication through these servers is real-time voice between connected users.
privacy.children.title: Children
privacy.children.text: VoxDMR is not directed at children under 13. The app requires a licensed amateur radio DMR ID to operate.
privacy.changes.title: Changes
privacy.changes.text: This privacy policy may be updated. Changes will be posted at this URL with an updated date.
privacy.contact.title: Contact
privacy.contact.text: If you have questions about this privacy policy, contact:
docs.platform.label: Show instructions for
